pi_items.source: Texas Instruments LM555 datasheet formula T=1.1×R×C verified with calculation: T = 1.1 × 470,000Ω × 0.0001F = 51.7 seconds. Independently verified on CircuitDigest and AllAboutCircuits 555 timer monostable calculators.
No authoritative source found specifying this tolerance value. Appears to be task design choice.
No authoritative source found specifying this tolerance value. Appears to be task design choice.
Confirmed in both setup_task.sh (line 6) and export_result.sh (line 10)
Task description and verifier.py prompt specify R = 470 kΩ
Task description and verifier.py prompt specify C = 100 µF
Task description explicitly requires switching to Monostable mode. Verifier.py checks for 'monostable_mode_selected'.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires calculating the output pulse width of a 555 timer IC in Monostable mode. The 555 timer is a real, widely-used integrated circuit (NE555/LM555) designed by Hans Camenzind in 1971. The pulse width formula for monostable mode is T = 1.1 × R × C (where T is in seconds, R in ohms, C in farads), as specified in the official Texas Instruments LM555 datasheet. With the specified input values of R = 470 kΩ and C = 100 µF, the calculation yields T = 1.1 × 470,000 × 0.0001 = 51.7 seconds, which matches the metadata's expected_value of 51.7 seconds. The result file path is /sdcard/tasks/555_timer_monostable/result.txt. The data is synthetic (calculated from the standard formula, not measured from physical circuits). The app used is 'Electrical Calculations' (com.hsn.electricalcalculations), a real app available on Google Play Store that provides electrical engineering calculations and conversions.
